text: Please tell us what was the education that most allowed you to begin understanding these electrical basics? author: @ffaubert1
text:
Excellent work!  

I use window silicone for nonskid feet. I put 4 dabs on the bottom, flip it over and then set it on some waxed paper. Once the silicone dries, you're all set. Cheap and cheerful.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
A1 is black. Such a good remark. I collect chess sets and bought a reverse on in turkey once. The box type, chess on the outside, backgammon on the inside.
And I have an african stone one i bought on a market in france. The guy wanted 90 euro and I did not want to spend that although i would say it was worth it.. He said "what would you give ?" I said 15 euro, just to get rid of the guy. "Sold !" he yells at me :-).

author: @darrellpidgeon6440
text: That's a nice set, with a modern design.  I love all the ways that people make a set out of such a variety of materials.  I used to fiddle with designs in the past, testing different things from electronic components to small plumbing parts.  I am also spoiled on having bases that are weighted with felt bottoms.  It just gives a better feel during a game.

author: @foley2k2
text: Cool idea. I happen to be 3d printing a voroni chess set now. It's going to take about 16 hours to get a full set at 50% scale.

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: I don’t mean to be negative but Laser cutters are very high tech, expensive machines, that 95% of us will never even get to see, let alone have easy access to. But I suppose for those who do it is a good way of reusing that plastic. But unless 100% renewable energy is used,.the saving in materials would be countered by the Carbon Footprint. From the large amount of energy needed to  run the Laser Cutter!.

author: @MerwinARTist

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @MerwinARTist
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering .. back when I was in the Army .. about 1973 .. I built a chess set that was made out of ceramic.  The king and queen were 8" tall.  The two sides had different eye, hair, clothes, etc. color.  They had sand inside to weigh them down .. and felt pads on the bottom.  I made a chess table for them also .. and it was lined with a raised cushion padding around so the pieces could not fall off easily .. it was a lot of detail work .. I played chess mostly in High School with a couple friends.  I realize some people have different viewpoints about what people do (making something) but there is only so much time you can spend on any one thing .. I think the design of your set was beautiful in it's simplicity and function. There are always things people can do to make something a bit better .. but there are 100's of other things to do too.  It's really fun and interesting to watch you work on brain tingling things!!  😊👍
